question: A REST outbound connector sends a POST with a JSON request body but its **HTTP headers** field is empty. Which `Content-Type` header does the connector send on the request?

options.text: `application/json` — the connector sets it automatically when no `Content-Type` header is configured.
`text/plain` — the standard default for any HTTP request.
No `Content-Type` is sent at all; the server must infer it from the body.

a.text: The REST connector docs state: "If you do not set the `Content-Type` header in your HTTP headers, the connector will automatically set the `Content-Type` to `application/json`."

b.text: There is no `text/plain` default in the REST connector. JSON is the implicit default because the connector's body model is JSON-first.

c.text: `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` is the browser-form default, not the REST connector default. JSON is what gets set when the header is missing.

d.text: The connector does not leave the header off — it injects `application/json` so the body is interpreted correctly by the receiver.

explanation: The REST connector treats JSON as the default body format. When the **HTTP headers** field does not include a `Content-Type`, the connector adds `Content-Type: application/json` automatically. Setting `Content-Type: multipart/form-data` changes the behavior — only document and string body fields are sent as multipart parts, and other fields (e.g., JSON objects) are ignored.
